title: English 12 Research Writing Comments
description: English 12 starter comment bank for research writing aligned to grades 11-12 2023 NJSLS-ELA references. These materials are teacher-editable starter review materials aligned to common high school ELA writing tasks and 2023 NJSLS-ELA references. They are not official state curriculum, not district grading policy, and not automatic evaluation.

categories.label: Research Question
categories.description: Reusable review items for research question.

categories.label: Source Quality
categories.description: Reusable review items for source quality.

categories.label: Source Integration
categories.description: Reusable review items for source integration.

categories.label: Academic Style
categories.description: Reusable review items for academic style.

categories.label: Revision Next Steps
categories.description: Reusable review items for revision next steps.

comments.label: Research Question strength
comments.short_text: Your research question sets up a focused question that can guide inquiry, which helps the reader understand the work ....
comments.text: Your research question sets up a focused question that can guide inquiry, which helps the reader understand the work with precision and attention to complexity.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen research question?
comments.revision_action: Keep this research question move and apply it in another paragraph or section.

comments.label: Research Question developing
comments.short_text: Your research question is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you narrow the question so it can be answere....
comments.text: Your research question is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you narrow the question so it can be answered with available sources.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen research question?
comments.revision_action: Narrow the question so it can be answered with available sources.

comments.label: Source Quality strength
comments.short_text: Your source quality uses credible sources that fit the purpose, which helps the reader understand the work with preci....
comments.text: Your source quality uses credible sources that fit the purpose, which helps the reader understand the work with precision and attention to complexity.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen source quality?
comments.revision_action: Keep this source quality move and apply it in another paragraph or section.

comments.label: Source Quality developing
comments.short_text: Your source quality is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you replace weak or mismatched sources with mor....
comments.text: Your source quality is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you replace weak or mismatched sources with more authoritative ones.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen source quality?
comments.revision_action: Replace weak or mismatched sources with more authoritative ones.

comments.label: Source Integration strength
comments.short_text: Your source integration introduces and explains source material smoothly, which helps the reader understand the work ....
comments.text: Your source integration introduces and explains source material smoothly, which helps the reader understand the work with precision and attention to complexity.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen source integration?
comments.revision_action: Keep this source integration move and apply it in another paragraph or section.

comments.label: Source Integration developing
comments.short_text: Your source integration is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you avoid dropped quotations by setting up ....
comments.text: Your source integration is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you avoid dropped quotations by setting up and analyzing source material.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen source integration?
comments.revision_action: Avoid dropped quotations by setting up and analyzing source material.

comments.label: Citation strength
comments.short_text: Your citation keeps source credit clear for the reader, which helps the reader understand the work with precision and....
comments.text: Your citation keeps source credit clear for the reader, which helps the reader understand the work with precision and attention to complexity.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen citation?
comments.revision_action: Keep this citation move and apply it in another paragraph or section.

comments.label: Citation developing
comments.short_text: Your citation is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you check citation details and distinguish source ide....
comments.text: Your citation is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you check citation details and distinguish source ideas from your own.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen citation?
comments.revision_action: Check citation details and distinguish source ideas from your own.

comments.label: Synthesis strength
comments.short_text: Your synthesis connects sources or texts into a larger insight, which helps the reader understand the work with preci....
comments.text: Your synthesis connects sources or texts into a larger insight, which helps the reader understand the work with precision and attention to complexity.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen synthesis?
comments.revision_action: Keep this synthesis move and apply it in another paragraph or section.

comments.label: Synthesis developing
comments.short_text: Your synthesis is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you explain what the sources or texts show together.
comments.text: Your synthesis is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you explain what the sources or texts show together.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen synthesis?
comments.revision_action: Explain what the sources or texts show together.

comments.label: Organization strength
comments.short_text: Your organization guides the reader through the ideas in a logical order, which helps the reader understand the work ....
comments.text: Your organization guides the reader through the ideas in a logical order, which helps the reader understand the work with precision and attention to complexity.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen organization?
comments.revision_action: Keep this organization move and apply it in another paragraph or section.

comments.label: Organization developing
comments.short_text: Your organization is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you reorder or group ideas so each paragraph has ....
comments.text: Your organization is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you reorder or group ideas so each paragraph has a clear job.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen organization?
comments.revision_action: Reorder or group ideas so each paragraph has a clear job.

comments.label: Academic Style strength
comments.short_text: Your academic style uses an academic style that supports credibility, which helps the reader understand the work with....
comments.text: Your academic style uses an academic style that supports credibility, which helps the reader understand the work with precision and attention to complexity.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen academic style?
comments.revision_action: Keep this academic style move and apply it in another paragraph or section.

comments.label: Academic Style developing
comments.short_text: Your academic style is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you revise vague wording and unsupported genera....
comments.text: Your academic style is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you revise vague wording and unsupported generalizations.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen academic style?
comments.revision_action: Revise vague wording and unsupported generalizations.

comments.label: Revision Next Steps strength
comments.short_text: Your revision next steps identifies a useful next revision move, which helps the reader understand the work with prec....
comments.text: Your revision next steps identifies a useful next revision move, which helps the reader understand the work with precision and attention to complexity.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen revision next steps?
comments.revision_action: Keep this revision next steps move and apply it in another paragraph or section.

comments.label: Revision Next Steps developing
comments.short_text: Your revision next steps is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you choose one high-impact revision target....
comments.text: Your revision next steps is on the right track, but it will be stronger if you choose one high-impact revision target before editing smaller details.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What is one place where you can strengthen revision next steps?
comments.revision_action: Choose one high-impact revision target before editing smaller details.

comments.label: Research Question hard to follow
comments.short_text: The research question is difficult to follow right now.
comments.text: The research question is difficult to follow right now. Revisit this part first so the reader can understand the purpose of the writing.
comments.teacher_note: Use when research question is a major barrier to understanding.
comments.follow_up_prompt: Where should the student begin revising research question?
comments.revision_action: Rewrite the section that most affects research question before polishing sentences.

comments.label: Source Quality hard to follow
comments.short_text: The source quality is difficult to follow right now.
comments.text: The source quality is difficult to follow right now. Revisit this part first so the reader can understand the purpose of the writing.
comments.teacher_note: Use when source quality is a major barrier to understanding.
comments.follow_up_prompt: Where should the student begin revising source quality?
comments.revision_action: Rewrite the section that most affects source quality before polishing sentences.

comments.label: Source Integration hard to follow
comments.short_text: The source integration is difficult to follow right now.
comments.text: The source integration is difficult to follow right now. Revisit this part first so the reader can understand the purpose of the writing.
comments.teacher_note: Use when source integration is a major barrier to understanding.
comments.follow_up_prompt: Where should the student begin revising source integration?
comments.revision_action: Rewrite the section that most affects source integration before polishing sentences.

comments.label: Citation hard to follow
comments.short_text: The citation is difficult to follow right now.
comments.text: The citation is difficult to follow right now. Revisit this part first so the reader can understand the purpose of the writing.
comments.teacher_note: Use when citation is a major barrier to understanding.
comments.follow_up_prompt: Where should the student begin revising citation?
comments.revision_action: Rewrite the section that most affects citation before polishing sentences.

comments.label: Academic Style next revision pass
comments.short_text: For the next revision pass, focus on academic style: Revise one moment so the explanation addresses significance, com....
comments.text: For the next revision pass, focus on academic style: Revise one moment so the explanation addresses significance, complexity, or audience impact.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What single revision move would most improve academic style?
comments.revision_action: Make one focused pass for academic style and then reread the full piece.

comments.label: Revision Next Steps next revision pass
comments.short_text: For the next revision pass, focus on revision next steps: Revise one moment so the explanation addresses significance....
comments.text: For the next revision pass, focus on revision next steps: Revise one moment so the explanation addresses significance, complexity, or audience impact.
comments.follow_up_prompt: What single revision move would most improve revision next steps?
comments.revision_action: Make one focused pass for revision next steps and then reread the full piece.

comments.label: Source integration synthesis bridge
comments.short_text: Add a synthesis sentence that weighs the source detail against another source, limitation, or implication.
comments.text: Add a synthesis sentence that weighs the source detail against another source, limitation, or implication.
comments.follow_up_prompt: Where can the student explain how a source changes or deepens the point?
comments.revision_action: Add a sentence after the source material that explains its relationship to the research question.

comments.label: Citation and source boundary check
comments.short_text: Check that the reader can tell which ideas come from sources and which ideas are yours.
comments.text: Check that the reader can tell which ideas come from sources and which ideas are yours. Add a citation or signal phrase anywhere that boundary is unclear.
comments.follow_up_prompt: Where might the reader be unsure whether an idea is sourced or original?
comments.revision_action: Review signal phrases, parenthetical citations, and source boundaries before final editing.

module_details.notice: These materials are teacher-editable starter review materials aligned to common high school ELA writing tasks and 2023 NJSLS-ELA references. They are not official state curriculum, not district grading policy, and not automatic evaluation.
module_details.emphasis: precise and knowledgeable claims, significance, logical sequencing of claims and counterclaims, strongest relevant evidence, avoidance of fallacies, synthesis, sophisticated style, audience awareness, complexity, ambiguity, nuance, and independent revision.
